title: drymocallis lactea var. lactea
intro: Drymocallis lactea var. lactea, also known as white goatsbeard, is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant's roots and leaves have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, particularly for their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which have been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The health benefits of white goatsbeard include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
